what_it_does: It is a top-level orientation document for Claude Code sessions inside the company repository, telling the agent that there are no human developers, which files to read first, the vault conventions that govern markdown notes, and how to use the external orienteering ledger for assets outside the repo.
observations: The repo is built and maintained entirely by AI agents, with no human developers.
AGENTS.md is declared the source of truth for rules, placement, and self-description discipline.
The recommended read order is AGENTS.md, then MAP.md, STATE.md, the touched module's AGENTS.md, and HANDOFF.md.
The repo is treated as both code and an Obsidian vault, with the same markdown serving as instructions and navigable self-model.
Vault conventions live in docs/vault-conventions.md and require frontmatter, alias links, typed relations, and a register of descriptive vs prescriptive.
Every folder must have an AGENTS.md constitution and MAP.md is the single vault home.
Status on terrain-entries defaults to unconfirmed and must not be asserted by inference; module build-state uses a separate living/building/planned vocabulary.
Terrain entries must carry coverage metadata and working docs keep their own frontmatter.
Company assets such as model engines, recall index, TLS certs, systemd services, and scattered data live outside ~/company and are mapped in orienteering/INDEX.md.
The orienteering map is explicitly provisional and not a blueprint.
standouts: AI-only developer premise with a constitution-first workflow.
The entire repo is modeled as a self-describing Obsidian knowledge vault with strict markdown ontology.
External assets are tracked in a separate orienteering ledger rather than being assumed to live inside the repo.
conventions: Follows the folder-note pattern: every folder has an AGENTS.md constitution and MAP.md is the vault home.
Follows strict self-description discipline: frontmatter, alias links, typed relations, register descriptive/prescriptive, confirmed-only status, and coverage tracking.
Breaks its own convention: this orientation file has no frontmatter, typed relations, or register/status/coverage fields.
concerns: The file mandates frontmatter and typed relations for self-description notes but does not include them itself.
It references many external files and concepts without providing their full paths or verification criteria within this file.
notes: A session-entry onboarding note that tells Claude Code which documents to read first, which vault conventions apply, and how to consult the orienteering ledger before touching anything outside the repo.
questions: What exactly qualifies a note as a terrain-entry versus a module build-state note?
How is the generated vault index produced and kept in sync?
What are the names and physical locations of the ~20 company-* systemd services?
What is the intended relationship between CLAUDE.md and the folder-note AGENTS.md constitutions?
What does the self-description discipline require beyond frontmatter and alias links?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is presented as an orientation for Claude Code sessions and it performs that role by listing prerequisite reads, conventions, and the external-asset ledger.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a ritualized onboarding path so any Claude Code session can locate the prescribed documents, avoid inferring live or dead state, and treat the repository as a navigable knowledge graph rather than a conventional codebase.
novelty: It treats markdown orientation files as a constitution, the repository as an Obsidian vault with a strict frontmatter ontology, and mandates an external orienteering ledger for assets that live outside the repo.
contribution: It contributes a session-entry checklist that names the documents and conventions an agent must consult before modifying code or notes, reducing arbitrary action.
summary_for_embedding: CLAUDE.md is the top-level orientation note for AI agents working in the company repository, prescribing the read-first document stack, vault-wide markdown conventions, and the use of an external orienteering ledger for out-of-repo assets.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to standardize how Claude Code sessions orient themselves and which documents and conventions to consult before acting.
